feed.title: SAP Community - SAP HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: SAP backup of data for a specific company code
entry.content:
There is a requirement to back up all SAP transactions on daily basis and keep the data physically in a specific country, including both manual and automatic entries.
Can the HANA database back up data for a specific company code? Or is there another way to achieve this?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: How an I traverse thru tables and fill a result table?
entry.content:
BusinessOne objects in SAP rest in a bunch of tables, that have a numeric ID but a text name - for whatever reason.
The first function just gets me the correct table:

CREATE FUNCTION "q_GetDocLinesTable"
/**
 * Get B1 objects table of a documents lines
 * V1.0 Coded 2026-02-05 by .rhavin;)
 */
(
	/* B1 Document Type */
	IN ObjType int
)
	/* B1 Document Meta Table */
	RETURNS TableName NVARCHAR(10)

	LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT 

AS

BEGIN

	IF     (:ObjType=13) THEN TableName := 'INV1'; /* A/R Invoice */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=14) THEN TableName := 'RIN1'; /* A/R Credit Memo */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=15) THEN TableName := 'DLN1'; /* Delivery */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=16) THEN TableName := 'RDN1'; /* Returns */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=17) THEN TableName := 'RDR1'; /* Sales Order */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=18) THEN TableName := 'PCH1'; /* A/P Invoice */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=19) THEN TableName := 'RPC1'; /* A/P Credit Memo */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=20) THEN TableName := 'PDN1'; /* Goods Receipt PO */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=21) THEN TableName := 'RPD1'; /* Goods Return */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=22) THEN TableName := 'POR1'; /* Purchase Order */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=23) THEN TableName := 'QUT1'; /* Sales Quotation */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=24) THEN TableName := 'RCT1'; /* Incoming Payment */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=67) THEN TableName := 'WTR1'; /* Incoming Payment */
	ELSEIF (:ObjType=540000006) THEN TableName := 'PQT1'; /* WTF? */
	ELSE TableName := NULL;
	END IF;
End;

I also created a type that I need to fill:

CREATE TYPE "q_tt_EntryMap" AS TABLE (
	"Entry" INTEGER,
	"Type" INTEGER
);

Now, what I want to is:

  • Step 1: Start with any given combination of Entry/Type
  • Step 2: fill a resulting table of type q_tt_EntryMap with the combination (if found)
  • Step 3: if any "BaseEntry": move to that combination and goto Step 2.

The following function gets me the first entry, but I'm unsure how to append to my result-table and how to move on to the next entry:

CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE "q_GetEntryTable"
(
EntryID int,
EntryType int
)

LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT 
SQL SECURITY INVOKER

AS
	LineTable     NVARCHAR(10);
	tSQL          NVARCHAR(4000);
	Result        "q_tt_EntryMap";

BEGIN

	LineTable := "q_GetDocLinesTable"(EntryType);
--	WHILE (:LineTable IS NOT NULL)
--	BEGIN
		tSQL := '
		SELECT DISTINCT "BaseEntry" AS "Entry", "BaseType" AS "Type"
		FROM ' || :LineTable || '
		WHERE "DocEntry" = ' || :EntryID || ' AND "BaseType" <> -1
		';
		EXECUTE IMMEDIATE tSQL into Result; /* ## How do I append? ## */
--		LineTable := "q_GetDocLinesTable"(/* ## Somehow put Type-Field of last row of :result into it ## */);


--	END;
		
	SELECT * FROM :Result;

END;

 

So if [1234,15] has a BaseEntry of [2345,13] and that has a BaseEntry of [3456,17] (I just made up those numbers), the resulting table returned should look like this:

(Entry)(Type)
123415
234513
345617

Of course, if there is a better way to do this, please feel free to tell me.

entry.title: Cross-Plant PPDS Planning: Special Procurement Not Triggering STR in MD01N
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue with MRP Live (MD01N) and PPDS planning for a cross-plant scenario where

Plant A → Material 123 (MRP Type: X0, Procurement: F, Special Procurement: 97 → Plant B , No BOM)
Plant B → Same Material 123 (MRP Type: X0, Procurement: E, BOM & PDS maintained)
Transportation Lane exists (Plant B → Plant A) “Creation from Special Procurement” is maintained
When dependent requirement (DepReq) created in Plant A and MRP Live is run, system should Create Stock Transfer / PR in Plant A . Generate requirement and planned order in Plant B

But No procurement proposal is generated in Plant A (no STR/PR) , No requirement is transferred to Plant B.

Does MRP Live (PPDS) support stock transfer using only Special Procurement (F+97)?
Is Source List mandatory for source determination in PPDS?
What is the correct standard approach to achieve this scenario?

Any guidance or best practices would be very helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: How do I USE an OUT-Table?
entry.content:
I have a procedure that has an OUT table variable:

CREATE OR REPLACE PROCEDURE "q_FollowLinkedTables"
(
  IN  EntryNumber int,
  IN  EntryType   int,
  IN  Backwards   BOOLEAN,
  OUT Result      "q_tt_EntryMap"
)

LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT 
SQL SECURITY INVOKER

AS
  LineTable    NVARCHAR(10);
  tSQL         NVARCHAR(4000);
  ActualTab    "q_tt_EntryMap";
  AllTab       "q_tt_EntryMap";
  ActualNumber int;
  ActualType   int;
  TypeField    NVARCHAR(10);
  NumberField  NVARCHAR(10);
  counter      int;

BEGIN
  counter      := 0;
  ActualNumber := EntryNumber;
  ActualType   := EntryType;
  IF :Backwards = TRUE THEN
    TypeField    := 'BaseType';
    NumberField  := 'BaseEntry';
  ELSE
    TypeField    := 'TargetType';
    NumberField  := 'TrgetEntry';
  END IF;
  LineTable    := "q_GetDocLinesTable"(EntryType);

  WHILE :LineTable IS NOT NULL
  DO
    counter := counter + 1;
    tSQL := '
    SELECT DISTINCT "'|| :NumberField ||'" AS "Entry", "'|| :TypeField ||'" AS "Type"
    FROM ' || :LineTable || '
    WHERE "DocEntry" = ' || :EntryNumber || ' AND "'|| :TypeField ||'" <> -1
    ';
    EXECUTE IMMEDIATE tSQL into ActualTab;
    IF IS_EMPTY(:ActualTab) THEN
      BREAK;
    END IF;
    
    SELECT "Type","Entry" INTO EntryType, EntryNumber FROM :ActualTab;

    INSERT INTO :AllTab VALUES (EntryNumber, EntryType);

    LineTable := "q_GetDocLinesTable"(EntryType);
    
    /* reasonable safeguard to prevent recursive locks */
    IF :counter > 10 THEN
      BREAK;
    END IF;

  END WHILE;

  Result = SELECT * FROM :AllTab;

END;

If i call this procedure in the HANA Studio console, I get the correct result:

But I need this in a variable in the calling procedure. When I do... 

CALL "q_FollowLinkedTables"(EntryNumber, EntryType, TRUE, :Result);

.. that leads to "Not supported combination of DML operations for variable" -what? How is this supposed to be done? I want to give this procedure a variable that I can further process in the calling procedure.

entry.title: RC 12 Error during Client Import to MXQ 500 / QAS 500 (Reopened)
entry.content: Continuing: How to run CHECK_TABLE_CONSISTENCY in SAP HANA Studio

entry.title: Question
entry.content: Can anyone tell me what is the use of Appending Table in SAP ABAP?

entry.title: SAP HANA Client Silent Install via SCCM Missing ODBC
entry.content:
Dear all,

We are currently facing an issue with the SAP HANA package deployment via SCCM. When we assign the package to a PC, it appears to install successfully through Software Center. However, the ODBC configuration is not set up after the installation.

If we uninstall the package and then reinstall, the ODBC configuration is created correctly. This behavior occurs consistently on every machine.

Has anyone encountered a similar issue or found a solution to ensure the ODBC configuration is properly applied during the initial installation? Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Extract data from supplier hub to HANA DB
entry.content:
We want to pull the data from the supplier Data Hub to our HANA Tables. What are the best options.

Currently they have given us login credentials where we can write a simple query and extract data to excel. or we can connect to power BI.

 

We want to bring that data to HANA tables, so we have all the historic data aswell in our DB

entry.title: Not able to connect with SAP HANA database customized table,error connection refused 10907 on SAPgui
entry.content:
Not able to connect with SAP HANA database customized table, error connection refused 10907 on SAP S/4 HANA GUI

While accessing the customized table content from SE11, or activating the table getting this error. All tables are giving this connection error. No one able to access db table in our company.

I tried on SAP HANA interactive terminal also.

Getting this same error:


* -10709: Connection failed (RTE:[89006] System call 'recv' failed, rc=10054:An existing connection was forcibly closed by the remote host {192.***.***.***:62342 -> ***.***.***.***:8042} (192.***.***.***:62342 -> ******.****.*****.**u:8042)) SQLSTATE: HY000


hdbsql

entry.title: Transparent huge pages are not enabled in HANA
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are getting EWA alert message Transparent huge pages are not enabled. I disabled it but when server rebooted it again enabled. How to disabled it permanently.

HANA version 2.0 SPS 07 2.00.079.02.1734604810 (fa/hana2sp07)

Linux version - SUSE 15 SP 5

Regards,

Akash

entry.title: S/4 HANA - different PRT behaviour in Fiori applications
entry.content:
Greetings,

we are having some issues with Fiori aopplications usied in EAM/PM/AM processes. We are experiencing different behaviour depending on which Fiori application we use to access maintenance order data. Focus is on management of production resources and tools (PRT).

We are wonding if anyone has had a similar issue and if we are perhaps missing some configuration. These are all standard applications without any enhancments.

1. APP Perform Maintenance Job - in the same row SAP states that there are no PRT while the total PRT count is 4

2. APP Manage Maintenance Orders - documents are not shown as PRT and it is not possible to add them in the order - the category D is miissing from dropdown list

3. APP Maintenance orders - all PRT are displayed correctly

Best regards, M.

entry.title: Large Technical Tables /AIF/PERS_XML
entry.content:
HI Team 

As part of RISE program SAP suggested the below recommendations

1.Transactions /AIF/MSG_DEL, /AIF/XML_COMPRESS , /AIF/PERS_DEL and report /AIF/MESSAGE_DELETION
2.Need to implement this SAP notes 2274361,2279909,3275559 to enable the required transactions-programs for compressing and deleting the XML messages
3.Only 3275559 note is revelent in our QAL system and can be implemented and the other 2 notes 2274361,2279909 are not applicable or not revelent
4.After applying the notes the given Tcodes, reports will be enabled.

Sine the given notes are not applicable could you please suggest the possible option or the correction notes to complet the recommendations

entry.title: Post REM Activities using RepetitiveMfgConfirmation API
entry.content:
Hello,

Is it possible to post REM Activities specific times using the RepetitiveMfgConfirmation API or any other API?
It is possible to do it with the transaction MFBF, but I cannot find it in the API documentation.

Thank you

Regards

Julien

entry.title: Migrating CRM Sales Orders & Service Contracts to SAP S/4HANA CE -Single Order Framework Feasibility
entry.content:
We are migrating from SAP CRM to S/4HANA Customer Engagement. We have three specific questions:

  • For CRM Sales Orders — is migration to S/4HANA SD (VA01) the only standard path, or is there any CE equivalent?
  • For Service Contracts originating in SD — should these migrate to CE Service Contracts (Fiori App F2358) or remain in SD?
  • In CRM we used a Single Order Framework covering both Sales and Service in one transaction. What is the recommended end-to-end process design in S/4HANA CE, where Sales is handled
  • outside of CE? How do others handle document flow continuity across SD and CE?

 

We need additional investigation on this topic. Any input from community members or SAP product experts on standard approaches, BTP integration patterns, or known gaps would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: NSE und DataAging Felder
entry.content:
Hello,
Is it possible to populate the DataAging fields of tables in the same way as with “dead” DataAging, and then use this field to perform partitioning via NSE without the drawbacks of DataAging?

We do not use DataAging, but would like to use NSE to evict data from RAM. However, most tables do not provide a suitable criterion.

Example: Workflows
SWP_JOIN
SWP_NODEWI
SWPNODE
SWPNODELOG
SWPSTEPLOG
SWWCNTP0
SWW_CONT
SWW_CONTOB
SWWLOGHIST
SWWFLEXPROC
SWWWICOMMENT
SWF_FLEX_COLL_AT

Only SWWLOGHIST has the METH_EDATE field as a suitable criterion.

We would like to partition all of the above tables using the DataAging fields and overwrite certain partitions via NSE. To do this, we would like to propagate the contents of SWWLOGHIST-METH_DATE (if necessary, using a custom program) to the DataAging fields of all involved tables (daily run).

Will we encounter any issues with this approach, or are the DataAging fields freely available as long as the DataAging business function DAAG_DATA_AGING is inactive?
Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

****************************************************************************
Hallo,

kann man die DataAging-Felder von Tabellen analog zum toten DataAging füllen, dann die Partitionierung per NSE über dieses Feld vornehmen, ohne die Nachteile von DataAging zu haben. 

Wir haben DataAging nicht im Einsatz, würden aber gerne Daten per NSE aus dem RAM verdrängen. Die meisten Tabellen stellen aber kein geeignetes Kriterium bereit.

Beispiel: Workflows
SWP_JOIN 
SWP_NODEWI  
SWPNODE 
SWPNODELOG  
SWPSTEPLOG  
SWWCNTP0  
SWW_CONT  
SWW_CONTOB  
SWWLOGHIST     
SWWFLEXPROC  
SWWWICOMMENT  
SWF_FLEX_COLL_AT  

Nur SWWLOGHIST hat als adäquates Kriterium das Feld METH_EDATE.

Wir würden gerne alle obigen Tabellen über die DataAging-Felder partitionieren und bestimme Partitionen per NSE verdrängen.  Hierzu würden wir gerne den Inhalt von SWWLOGHIST-METH_DATE (zur Not auch mit einem eigenen Programm) in die DataAgingfelder aller beteiligen Tabellen vererben (täglicher Lauf). 

Bekommen wir bei dieser Vorgehensweise ein Problem oder stehen die DataAging-Felder frei zu Verfügung, solange die Businessfunction DAAG_DATA_AGING des DataAging inaktiv ist?

entry.title: Table names and string translation
entry.content: Table "ORDR" contains all orders, table "ODLN" all delivery notices. But where do I get the string "Order" or "Delivery" and where are the translations stored, for example the German string "Lieferung"?

entry.title: BaseRefs and TargetEntries in lines instead of documents
entry.content:
Table "ODLN" holds the delivery notes data, table "DLN1" the lines/items in the delivery. Yet the ODLN has "BaseRef/BaseType" of -1 while the corresponding "DLN1" has the actual values and no field "TrgtEntry/TargetType". But the table DLN1 has. So, why is this and does that mean that a delivery note can have multiple parallel sales orders of different items?

How does this work, I cant figure out in B1 how to do this without changing the single base document.

entry.title: Clean Core Compliance – Handling Dynamic WHERE and Dynamic Table Names in ABAP
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are currently working on aligning our custom ABAP developments with Clean Core / ABAP Cloud guidelines, and we encountered ATC warnings related to the following patterns:

  1. Usage of dynamic WHERE conditions in Open SQL
  2. Usage of dynamic table/view names in Open SQL

As per Clean Core principles, these constructs are discouraged due to potential concerns around security, stability, and upgrade safety.

Our Current Approach

To address these warnings while preserving required flexibility, we implemented validation using standard class methods:

  • cl_abap_dyn_prg=>check_column_name( ) — for validating dynamic WHERE clause fields
  • cl_abap_dyn_prg=>check_table_or_view_name_str( ) — for validating dynamic table/view names

After introducing these validations, the ATC warnings are no longer triggered.

Questions

We would appreciate guidance from the community on the following:

  1. Compliance Clarification
    Is the use of CL_ABAP_DYN_PRG validation methods considered fully compliant with Clean Core principles, or does it merely act as a workaround to suppress ATC warnings?
  2. Recommended Approach for Dynamic Scenarios
    In cases where dynamic behavior is functionally required (e.g., configurable providers, metadata-driven frameworks, generic handlers), what is SAP’s recommended best practice under Clean Core guidelines?
  3. Alternative APIs / Frameworks
    Are there any released and upgrade-safe APIs or frameworks (especially in ABAP Cloud) that can replace such dynamic constructs?

Objective

Our goal is not just to suppress warnings but to ensure that our implementation aligns with SAP’s long-term vision for upgrade-safe, extensible, and compliant development.

Any insights, official documentation references, SAP Notes, or practical recommendations would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support!
Best regards,
Sunil

entry.title: Clean Core Alternative for Function Module [TRINT_READ_COMM_KEYS]
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 We are working on Clean Core compliance for our SAP S/4HANA 2023 On-Premise system. During ATC (ABAP Test Cockpit) checks, the function module [TRINT_READ_COMM_KEYS ] was flagged as internal/unreleased.

Current Usage: This function module is currently being used in our custom code,

Request: We would like to understand:

  1. Whether there are any released APIs or function modules available as alternatives to [TRINT_READ_COMM_KEYS]
  2. If there is a recommended Clean Core-compliant approach to achieve the same functionality
  3. Whether there are plans to release this function module or provide an alternative in future releases

System Details:

  • Product: SAP S/4HANA 2023 On-Premise
  • Purpose: Clean Core transformation and ATC compliance

We appreciate your assistance in this matter.

Best regards,
Sunil Hosur

entry.title: Clean Core Alternative for Class(CL_HARD_WIRED_ENCRYPTOR)
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are working on Clean Core compliance for our SAP S/4HANA 2023 On-Premise system. During ATC (ABAP Test Cockpit) checks, the Class [CL_HARD_WIRED_ENCRYPTOR] was flagged as internal/unreleased.

Current Usage: This function module is currently being used in our custom code 

Request: We would like to understand:

  1. Whether there are any released APIs or function modules available as alternatives to [CL_HARD_WIRED_ENCRYPTOR]
  2. If there is a recommended Clean Core-compliant approach to achieve the same functionality
  3. Whether there are plans to release this function module or provide an alternative in future releases

System Details:

  • Product: SAP S/4HANA 2023 On-Premise
  • Purpose: Clean Core transformation and ATC compliance

We appreciate your assistance in this matter.

Best regards,
Sunil Hosur
